text: The aim of this paper is to investigate the problem of choosing classifiers and regression models with low variance using cross-validation for being applicable to QSAR. State-of-the-art methods that were recently proposed can lead to highly variant models which cannot be used in many situations. The best practices to improve the reliability of model choice and thereby to obtain more reliable models is presented and examined. An essential element of the proposed strategies is cross-validation which appears to be a very efficient tool in this context.
